national_expectations.summary: Simply reaching the knockout round is already framed nationally as a triumph that exceeds pre-tournament hopes, since Bafana Bafana had never escaped the group stage in any prior appearance. Coach Broos has said the squad now wants more, and he openly calls a win over Canada that would carry them into the next round a miracle for the team. Previews paint South Africa as defensive underdogs and a wild card rather than favourites, so the bar is to keep the historic run alive while any further progress is treated as a bonus.

national_mood.summary: The mood is jubilant and proud after the win over South Korea sealed a first-ever knockout berth, with reports of overnight street celebrations and fans staying awake for the early-morning kickoff. Coverage describes a swing from pre-match pessimism and harsh criticism to nationwide celebration, with supporters calling it an incredibly proud moment and the government praising the team's determination, discipline and fighting spirit. Heading into Canada there is a sense of belief and momentum tempered by an underdog's realism, with the achievement framed as a watershed for South African football.

manager_situation.summary: Hugo Broos, the veteran Belgian coach who has led South Africa since 2021, stands hugely elevated after delivering the country's first knockout-stage qualification, an achievement that eluded his predecessors. He had long said the World Cup would close out his decades-long career so he could be closer to his family, but after reaching the last 32 he is publicly reconsidering, hinting he could find a less demanding role in South Africa so he can still contribute while spending more time with family. He has also hit back combatively at critics who doubted his side after the Mexico defeat, while keeping the team focused on chasing a further historic step against Canada.

historical_context.summary: This is South Africa's fourth World Cup appearance and the first time they have ever advanced beyond the group stage. They debuted at France in 1998 with players like Lucas Radebe and Benni McCarthy, returned in 2002 where they recorded a first World Cup win over Slovenia but went out on goal difference, and hosted the tournament in 2010, where despite a famous opening goal and a win over France they became the first host nation in World Cup history to fall in the group stage. The country rejoined international football in the early 1990s after the apartheid-era isolation, making the team a symbol of the post-apartheid nation, so finally reaching a knockout round carries weight well beyond sport.

sources.outlet: Wikipedia (South Africa at the FIFA World Cup)

dominant_storylines.storyline: The historic first: every preview leads on Bafana Bafana reaching a World Cup knockout round for the first time ever, recovering from an opening loss to hosts Mexico with a draw against Czechia (a late Mokoena penalty) and a win over South Korea (a Maseko goal) to finish runner-up in Group A. It is framed as a watershed for South African football and a milestone Broos achieved where past coaches failed.

Broos's future and his clash with critics: having long planned to retire after this World Cup to be closer to family, the 74-year-old is now openly considering a U-turn to stay in South Africa in some capacity, while also defiantly answering doubters who questioned his team after the Mexico defeat. This personal narrative dominates the build-up alongside the football.

Thapelo Maseko, the redemption hero: the young winger who scored the decisive group-stage goal is the breakout story, having lost his place at his club and gone through a long spell without a start before a loan move revived him; he is cast as South Africa's match-winner and youngest World Cup goalscorer heading into Canada.

Squad availability for the knockout: confirmed that attacker Themba Zwane is suspended after his red card against Mexico, with South Africa having lost their appeal against an extension of the ban, while key midfielder Teboho Mokoena returns from a one-match yellow-card suspension to anchor the engine room against Canada.

attributed_quotes.quote: It's historic. But I'm very happy for the guys.
attributed_quotes.context: Broos reacting after the 1-0 win over South Korea sealed South Africa's first-ever World Cup knockout qualification.

attributed_quotes.quote: If we should succeed again to go through to the round, I can say that should be a miracle.
attributed_quotes.context: Broos previewing the Round of 32 against Canada, framing further progress as beyond expectations. Quote lightly trimmed from '...go through to the (next) round, I can say that should be a miracle for Bafana Bafana' to under 20 words without altering meaning.

gaps: No reported or uncertain (unconfirmed) fitness doubts were found for any South Africa player; previews stated there were no injury concerns beyond suspensions, so local_availability_reporting is left empty.

The News24 page cited for Broos's retirement U-turn returned HTTP 403 and could not be read directly; the claim and the verbatim quote ('Maybe, maybe, we can find something in South Africa where I have to spend less time there, but I can contribute while also spending time with my family') are corroborated by an accessible Goal.com article and the News24 search summary, but the precise sentence was not used as an attributed_quote since the primary page was unreadable.

Minor source discrepancy: one early ESPN snippet rendered the Czechia match as a 2-1 South Africa win, but the authoritative reporting (Daily Maverick, Al Jazeera, ESPN match page) and the DB FACTS confirm it was a 1-1 draw via a late Mokoena penalty; the DB result (W1 D1 L1) was trusted.
Could not retrieve full FIFA official article text to add an official-tier confirmation of group results; all sourcing here is media/discovery tier.

The specific naming of Carlos Alberto Parreira as a predecessor who failed to reach the knockouts could not be confirmed in any cited source, so the manager_situation block was softened to reference 'predecessors' generally.
Olympics.com corroborating source for the historic-first storyline timed out on re-fetch; the storyline is fully supported by the Daily Maverick source that was read in full.
